desired_target.why: last-year etcd stable release in the 3.5 line; upstream release planning and go.mod indicate Go 1.24.x tooling instead of main branch Go 1.26
desired_target.materialization_status: not available in this offline machine state; local full clone only contains tags up to v3.5.9

actual_exercised_target.why: closest locally available release tag in the same 3.5 stable line, exported reproducibly with scripts/prepare-etcd-probe-target.sh

package_probe.top_failure_reason: requires go version 1.19 through 1.25, got go1.26

file_probe.methodology: standalone-file build of every .go file, including tests and non-main packages

package_probe.top_failure_reason: logger.go:24:2: module lookup disabled by GOPROXY=off

file_probe.methodology: package-context attribution with library-package stubs; test files omitted instead of forced-failed

improvements: The hard main-branch blocker changed from "requires go version 1.19 through 1.25, got go1.26" to actual package/dependency-level outcomes on an older release line.
Package probe moved from 0 successful packages on main to 26 successful packages on the older release-line rerun.
File probe moved from 14 raw standalone-file successes on main to 47 package-context-attributed non-test file successes, with 314 test files separated out instead of being mixed into failures.
remaining_limitations: This machine is offline for module resolution; many remaining failures are now "module lookup disabled by GOPROXY=off", which is an environment limit rather than a TinyGo semantic failure.
The exact 2025 target v3.5.25 is identified but not materialized locally, so the exercised rerun uses v3.5.9 from the same stable line as an offline fallback.
The new file probe is intentionally not comparable 1:1 with the old standalone-file probe because it corrects a major methodology distortion.
